segments.text: Jacob heard Laban’s sons’ words, saying,
Jacob has taken away all that was our father’s. He has obtained all this wealth from that which was our father’s.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Jacob saw the expression on Laban’s face, and, behold, it was not toward him as before.
Yahweh said to Jacob,
Return to the land of your fathers, and to your relatives, and I will be with you.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: Jacob sent and called Rachel and Leah to the field to his flock,
and said to them,
I see the expression on your father’s face, that it is not toward me as before; but the God of my father has been with me.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: You know that I have served your father with all of my strength.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: Your father has deceived me, and changed my wages ten times, but God didn’t allow him to hurt me.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: If he said, ‘The speckled will be your wages,’ then all the flock bore speckled. If he said, ‘The streaked will be your wages,’ then all the flock bore streaked.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: Thus God has taken away your father’s livestock, and given them to me.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: During mating season, I lifted up my eyes, and saw in a dream, and behold, the male goats which leaped on the flock were streaked, speckled, and grizzled.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: The angel of God said to me in the dream, ‘Jacob,’ and I said, ‘Here I am.’
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: He said, ‘Now lift up your eyes, and behold, all the male goats which leap on the flock are streaked, speckled, and grizzled, for I have seen all that Laban does to you.
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: I am the God of Bethel, where you anointed a pillar, where you vowed a vow to me. Now arise, get out from this land, and return to the land of your birth.’
segments.speaker: Yahweh

segments.text: Rachel and Leah answered him,
Is there yet any portion or inheritance for us in our father’s house?
segments.speaker: Rachel and Leah

segments.text: Aren’t we considered as foreigners by him? For he has sold us, and has also used up our money.
segments.speaker: Rachel and Leah

segments.text: For all the riches which God has taken away from our father are ours and our children’s. Now then, whatever God has said to you, do.
segments.speaker: Rachel and Leah

segments.text: Then Jacob rose up, and set his sons and his wives on the camels,
and he took away all his livestock, and all his possessions which he had gathered, including the livestock which he had gained in Paddan Aram, to go to Isaac his father, to the land of Canaan.
Now Laban had gone to shear his sheep; and Rachel stole the teraphim that were her father’s.
Jacob deceived Laban the Syrian, in that he didn’t tell him that he was running away.
So he fled with all that he had. He rose up, passed over the River, and set his face toward the mountain of Gilead.
Laban was told on the third day that Jacob had fled.
He took his relatives with him, and pursued him seven days’ journey. He overtook him in the mountain of Gilead.
God came to Laban the Syrian in a dream of the night, and said to him,
Be careful that you don’t speak to Jacob either good or bad.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Laban caught up with Jacob. Now Jacob had pitched his tent in the mountain, and Laban with his relatives encamped in the mountain of Gilead.
Laban said to Jacob,
What have you done, that you have deceived me, and carried away my daughters like captives of the sword?
segments.speaker: Laban

segments.text: Why did you flee secretly, and deceive me, and didn’t tell me, that I might have sent you away with mirth and with songs, with tambourine and with harp;
segments.speaker: Laban

segments.text: and didn’t allow me to kiss my sons and my daughters? Now you have done foolishly.
segments.speaker: Laban

segments.text: It is in the power of my hand to hurt you, but the God of your father spoke to me last night, saying, ‘Be careful that you don’t speak to Jacob either good or bad.’
segments.speaker: Laban

segments.text: Now, you want to be gone, because you greatly longed for your father’s house, but why have you stolen my gods?
segments.speaker: Laban

segments.text: Jacob answered Laban,
Because I was afraid, for I said, ‘Lest you should take your daughters from me by force.’
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Anyone you find your gods with shall not live. Before our relatives, discern what is yours with me, and take it.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: For Jacob didn’t know that Rachel had stolen them.
Laban went into Jacob’s tent, into Leah’s tent, and into the tent of the two female servants; but he didn’t find them. He went out of Leah’s tent, and entered into Rachel’s tent.
Now Rachel had taken the teraphim, put them in the camel’s saddle, and sat on them. Laban felt around all the tent, but didn’t find them.
She said to her father,
Don’t let my lord be angry that I can’t rise up before you; for I’m having my period.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: He searched, but didn’t find the teraphim.
Jacob was angry, and argued with Laban. Jacob answered Laban,
What is my trespass? What is my sin, that you have hotly pursued me?
segments.speaker: Jacob was angry, and

segments.text: Now that you have felt around in all my stuff, what have you found of all your household stuff? Set it here before my relatives and your relatives, that they may judge between us two.
segments.speaker: Jacob was angry, and

segments.text: “These twenty years I have been with you. Your ewes and your female goats have not cast their young, and I haven’t eaten the rams of your flocks.
segments.speaker: Jacob was angry, and

segments.text: That which was torn of animals, I didn’t bring to you. I bore its loss. Of my hand you required it, whether stolen by day or stolen by night.
segments.speaker: Jacob was angry, and

segments.text: This was my situation: in the day the drought consumed me, and the frost by night; and my sleep fled from my eyes.
segments.speaker: Jacob was angry, and

segments.text: These twenty years I have been in your house. I served you fourteen years for your two daughters, and six years for your flock, and you have changed my wages ten times.
segments.speaker: Jacob was angry, and

segments.text: Unless the God of my father, the God of Abraham, and the fear of Isaac, had been with me, surely now you would have sent me away empty. God has seen my affliction and the labor of my hands, and rebuked you last night.
segments.speaker: Jacob was angry, and

segments.text: Laban answered Jacob,

The daughters are my daughters, the children are my children, the flocks are my flocks, and all that you see is mine! What can I do today to these my daughters, or to their children whom they have borne?
segments.speaker: Laban

segments.text: Now come, let’s make a covenant, you and I. Let it be for a witness between me and you.
segments.speaker: Laban

segments.text: Jacob took a stone, and set it up for a pillar.
Jacob said to his relatives,
Gather stones.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: They took stones, and made a heap. They ate there by the heap.
Laban called it Jegar Sahadutha,  but Jacob called it Galeed.
Laban said,
This heap is witness between me and you today.
segments.speaker: Laban

segments.text: Therefore it was named Galeed
and Mizpah, for he said,
Yahweh watch between me and you, when we are absent one from another.
segments.speaker: Laban

segments.text: If you afflict my daughters, or if you take wives in addition to my daughters, no man is with us; behold, God is witness between me and you.
segments.speaker: Laban

segments.text: Laban said to Jacob,
See this heap, and see the pillar, which I have set between me and you.
segments.speaker: Laban

segments.text: May this heap be a witness, and the pillar be a witness, that I will not pass over this heap to you, and that you will not pass over this heap and this pillar to me, for harm.
segments.speaker: Laban

segments.text: The God of Abraham, and the God of Nahor, the God of their father, judge between us.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Then Jacob swore by the fear of his father, Isaac.
Jacob offered a sacrifice in the mountain, and called his relatives to eat bread. They ate bread, and stayed all night in the mountain.
Early in the morning, Laban rose up, and kissed his sons and his daughters, and blessed them. Laban departed and returned to his place.
